frozen_question: In one prospectively selected minimal-risk sandboxed review domain, can a preregistered control-envelope gate identify ceremonial approval episodes and improve the joint known-defect, correct-intervention, useful-throughput, burden, privacy, accessibility, and unequal-impact frontier over a strong static risk-tiered oversight baseline?

ethics_and_privacy_gate.deception_policy: Only if scientifically necessary, specifically approved, sandbox-bounded, promptly debriefed, and compatible with withdrawal or deletion obligations.

task_domain_candidates: sandboxed software-change review with independently seeded functional and policy defects
sandboxed incident triage with time-to-irreversibility and reversible intervention controls
sandboxed document or data-release review with known privacy and provenance defects
selection_before_outcomes.population_boundary: Adults with prospectively defined domain competence; pilot and held-out cohorts remain disjoint; convenience samples cannot support population-wide claims.
selection_before_outcomes.selection_rule: Use pilot-only task competence, known-effect sensitivity, intervention reachability, accessibility, evaluator agreement, variance, burden, privacy, and resource feasibility; freeze one domain, participant profile, interface, automation version, manipulation set, and denominator before held-out recruitment.
forbidden_selection_inputs: P2/Q1/Q2 tasks, labels, outcomes, evaluators, or retry decisions

arms.role: unassisted or minimally assisted review where feasible
arms.required_competence: task and time floors without automation-induced confounds

arms.role: nominal click approval with ordinary information
arms.required_competence: instrument sensitivity comparator only; never a preferred deployment baseline

arms.role: strong static risk-tiered oversight with fixed information, escalation, and safe-hold rules
arms.required_competence: known-defect, correct-intervention, burden, and useful-throughput floors

arms.role: versioned control-envelope gate with workload, time, representation, authority, intervention, conflict, privacy, and responsibility routing
arms.required_competence: all gate activations, safe alternatives, and no-authority boundaries observable before held-out opening

manipulations: concurrent review load and interruption burden
time window relative to independently implemented irreversibility
explanation presence, fidelity, and fluent-misleading mismatch
automation confidence including false high confidence
alert quality including flooding and stale evidence
mode visibility and visible mode mismatch
intervention cost, disabled intervention, and system response latency
competence_gates: Exact task, automation, interface, intervention, safe-state, ground-truth, evaluator, randomization, participant-profile, manipulation, cost, and analysis identities are frozen before held-out recruitment.
Independent task truth contains known seeded defects and consequence labels unavailable to the automation, interface authors, participants, and primary scorer until the appropriate episode stage.
All arms pass prospectively defined task usability and baseline floors under matched task information except the registered manipulations; the static baseline is tuned on pilot data only.

Participants receive domain-relevant training, current qualification checks, accessibility accommodations, comprehension practice, withdrawal rights, and compensation independent of agreement or speed.
Obvious-defect, impossible-window, disabled-channel, visible-mode-mismatch, and faithful-explanation positive controls pass frozen sensitivity floors.
The independent evaluator recovers frozen ground truth and intervention consequences with preregistered agreement while blinded to treatment and participant confidence where feasible.

The gate demonstrably changes routes for overload, missing authority, incompatible representation, late intervention, unavailable safe state, unmanaged conflict, and responsibility beyond effective control without granting support or effect authority.

Power, clustering, multiplicity, exclusions, missingness, attrition, subgroup analyses, adverse events, privacy incidents, rescues, retries, and the one-time held-out opening are frozen and fully retained.

Ethics, privacy, data-protection, accessibility, security, and institutional or jurisdictional approvals are current and authorize the exact recruitment, collection, retention, deception, debrief, and deletion plan.
positive_controls: faithful explanation expected to improve one narrow comprehension item
adversarial_controls: high test-pass signal with a hidden defect
nominal authority without a viable action
rescue_ladder: audit ground truth, randomization, logging, evaluator independence, and treatment masking
repair accessibility, instructions, training, interface, or task defects using pilot data only
verify every manipulation changed the intended condition without introducing an uncontrolled confound
rerun positive controls on a new pilot cohort without inspecting held-out outcomes
revise the competence dossier and freeze a new campaign identity before any fresh held-out recruitment
stop as ethics-, privacy-, instrument-, construct-, baseline-, power-, population-, accessibility-, or resource-inadequate rather than infer efficacy or impossibility
outcomes: safe useful throughput with holds, abstentions, and displaced work retained
calibrated reliance under true and false confidence
workload, interruption, training, and review burden
abstention, escalation, appeal, and correction quality
accessibility and subgroup burden without population-wide extrapolation
participant time, analyst time, compute, false-hold, false-intervention, recovery, and residual cost

decision_rule.positive: The envelope arm passes all competence, ethics, privacy, accessibility, burden, and non-inferiority gates and improves the frozen joint known-defect/correct-intervention/useful-throughput frontier over the strong static baseline with uncertainty excluding the minimum useful effect; no single favorable score may average away privacy, unequal burden, false intervention, or unsafe outcomes.
decision_rule.negative_exact: Every competence and ethical gate passes, rescue is exhausted prospectively, the study is powered for the minimum useful effect, and the frozen envelope arm fails the joint rule against the tuned static baseline.
decision_rule.mixed: Ceremonial episodes are detected but throughput, false intervention, privacy, accessibility, subgroup burden, or residual harm worsens; deployment remains blocked and axes remain separate.
decision_rule.inconclusive: Any human, task, interface, baseline, instrument, ground-truth, evaluator, ethics, privacy, accessibility, power, population, identity, isolation, or resource gate fails after bounded rescue.
decision_rule.maximum_negative_level: N3 for the exact frozen task domain, participant population, interface, automation, evaluator, intervention, manipulations, time, and resource regime only; meaningful control in general cannot be refuted.

resource_and_isolation_gate.execution_trigger: Open only after exact ethics and jurisdictional approval, competence dossier, power analysis, independent ground truth/evaluator, accessibility/privacy plans, participant budget, resource floor, and sealed denominator manifest pass.

artifact_contract: immutable ethics, consent, privacy, accessibility, recruitment, compensation, task, automation, interface, evaluator, intervention, and analysis manifests
sealed participant-profile, task, manipulation, randomization, pilot, held-out, and subgroup digests
one human oversight control packet per episode with pseudonymous identity and minimized fields
all decisions, interventions, system responses, independent outcomes, holds, abstentions, failures, exclusions, attrition, rescues, adverse events, complaints, appeals, and deletions
positive/adversarial control, evaluator agreement, masking, manipulation-check, and leakage ledgers
separate useful-throughput, defect, intervention, time, burden, privacy, accessibility, unequal-impact, cost, and residual estimates
retention and deletion receipts without participant content in the public repository
accepted or rejected evidence-transition record with N0-N5 classification and exact population/task ceiling
non_claims: Protocol readiness is not a human-subjects, comprehension, workload, trust, intervention, privacy, responsibility, safety, or meaningful-control result.
No participant is recruited or studied, and no ethics approval is inferred or bypassed by this repository record.
No support, effect authority, readiness, release, deployment, SOTA, AGI, or ASI movement is authorized.
